rag_rules: AGREE contracts must be placed in annex blocks within component type or implementation scopes, with type scopes for interface behavior and implementation scopes for subcomponent bindings.
AGREE assumptions and guarantees referencing ports must be in valid scopes; implementation-level bindings are only allowed in implementation annexes.
generic_rules: AGREE annex blocks must use only visible identifiers from the AADL model; no package-qualified names or undefined references are allowed.
AGREE expressions must not assume AADL properties are automatically available as variables; explicit typed ports or constants are required for timing or data constraints.
AGREE clauses should avoid unsafe numeric or Boolean encodings of event ports or opaque data types unless model-provided values are available.
Component type contracts should reference only features visible at the type scope; implementation-level guarantees must use valid subcomponent paths or outputs.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annex blocks are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` and must follow valid AGREE syntax for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and assignments.
Use exact AADL identifiers and avoid introducing new package or data type references not present in the model.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level bindings in component type annexes or reference subcomponent paths in type-level contracts.
Do not encode AADL properties like Period or Deadline as AGREE variables unless typed ports or constants are explicitly defined in the model.
irrelevant_topics: AADL property associations and their automatic availability in AGREE scope
Model fusion and architecture redesign during repair
Validator diagnostics and repair strategies beyond syntax and scope
